title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
On a police launch departing Kaukau Bay, Ellery Quin, Vance, Isam, and Professor Yardley witness the body of Stephen Megalas being lowered from the radio room roof of the yacht *Sea Hawk*. Captain Swift and Dr. Temple are present at the scene. Following this, Ellery expresses frustration, noting that the killer, Vilga Crocetta, remains an unidentified "ghost."

The narrative shifts to Bradwood in West Palm Beach, Florida. Ellery and Professor Yardley arrive at the residence, where they encounter a scene of grief involving Fox, Helen, and Bradwood's hysterical wife. After being admitted by Old Nanny, the two men retreat to the professor's study. Ellery struggles to grasp a fleeting, important idea that continues to elude him. To assist, Professor Yardley suggests that verbalizing thoughts or discussing unrelated matters might help the idea emerge.

The professor encourages Ellery to connect the testimony of Stolinsky with the specific arrangement of the chess pieces in Bradwood's study. They discuss the hypothesis that Bradwood was playing chess against himself at the time of his death. Professor Yardley proposes two unverified theories: either the killer, Crocetta, was an expected visitor whom Bradwood believed to be a friend, or there were two separate visitors that night—one expected and one being the enemy, Crocetta. Ellery rejects the possibility of there being only one visitor, arguing that the truth must lie in the specific arrangement of the chess pieces and the psychology of skilled players.
